description:
Production observability dashboard for job-worker. Covers consumer throughput and lag, listener latency, custom job outcomes, Redis latency, and runtime capacity.

How to use this dashboard: Start from the top Overview row to verify SLO health, then drill into throughput, latency, errors, and capacity sections to isolate bottlenecks. Use the template filters (environment, instance, topic, job type, endpoint, and command where available) to narrow scope during incidents and compare behavior across dimensions. Panel info icon meaning: Click the i icon in each panel header to see what the metric represents, how to interpret normal vs abnormal values, and what operational signal it provides.

panels.description: Successful jobs over selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs Consumed Success (range)

panels.description: Current successful job processing rate.
panels.title: Success Rate

panels.description: Kafka records consumed per second.
panels.title: Kafka Consumed Rate

panels.description: Maximum consumer lag.
panels.title: Max Lag

panels.description: Malformed payload percentage.
panels.title: Malformed %

panels.description: Target availability.
panels.title: Availability
Worker Consumption Flow
panels.description: Shows end-to-end worker intake health by comparing successful consumption, malformed payload detection, and Kafka consumed rate. Use this to confirm the worker is processing messages at expected volume and to spot ingestion anomalies quickly.

panels.title: Consumed Rate by Topic and Job Type
panels.description: Breaks down successful consumption throughput by Kafka topic and business job type. Use this panel to identify traffic distribution shifts, hot partitions or job classes, and unexpected drops for specific workloads.

panels.title: Lag by Topic (Current and Max)
panels.description: Tracks both current consumer lag and lag max per topic. Current lag reflects present backlog; max lag highlights worst observed pressure. Rising values indicate consumer throughput is not keeping up with producer rate.

panels.title: Consumer Efficiency
panels.description: Shows byte throughput and fetch efficiency metrics (records per request and fetch size). Use this to validate poll efficiency and identify under-fetched or oversized fetch patterns that can impact latency and broker load.

panels.title: Kafka Listener Latency (p50/p90/p95/p99)
panels.description: Displays Kafka listener execution latency percentiles. p50 indicates typical processing time, while p95 and p99 expose tail latency and outliers. Persistent tail growth usually signals downstream bottlenecks or contention.

panels.title: Listener Throughput and Mean Latency
panels.description: Correlates listener throughput (messages per second) with mean listener latency. Use this to detect saturation scenarios where throughput plateaus while average processing latency continues to rise.

panels.title: Kafka Consumer Internal Latency
panels.description: Shows internal Kafka consumer latency signals such as fetch and commit timing. Elevated values suggest consumer-side coordination or broker interaction delays that can contribute to lag accumulation.

panels.title: Redis Lettuce Latency
panels.description: Displays Redis command latency percentiles from precomputed Lettuce recording rules. Compare p50 to p99 to understand tail behavior; widening spread usually indicates network jitter, Redis pressure, or command-level hotspots.

panels.title: Outcome Error Signal
panels.description: Compares malformed outcome rate against overall outcome rate to quantify ingestion quality degradation over time. Use this as an early warning for serialization, schema, or producer payload regressions.

panels.title: Lag Growth by Topic
panels.description: Shows the slope of lag max over time per topic. Positive sustained growth indicates backlog is accelerating; values near zero indicate stable consumption; negative values indicate backlog recovery.

panels.title: Outcome Counts by Job Type
panels.description: Tabular view of consumed outcome totals by job type and result over the selected window. Use this for quick reconciliation of success vs malformed counts and to support incident triage.

panels.title: DLQ Producer Path
panels.description: Tracks worker-to-DLQ producer behavior including send, error, and retry rates. Use this to validate DLQ routing health and identify failures in the fallback path during malformed or failed processing bursts.

panels.title: JVM Memory
panels.description: Shows heap and non-heap JVM memory utilization for worker instances. Use this to monitor memory pressure trends, detect leak-like growth, and correlate GC behavior with throughput and latency changes.

panels.title: Process RSS and CPU
panels.description: Displays process resident memory and CPU utilization. Use this to identify host-level resource saturation, runaway memory growth, or CPU spikes affecting message processing stability.

panels.title: Threads, GC, Uptime
panels.description: Combines runtime stability indicators: live thread count, GC pause tail latency, and process uptime. Useful for detecting thread leaks, GC pause regressions, and frequent restarts.

panels.title: Redis Lettuce Throughput
panels.description: Shows command throughput rate for Redis operations by command type. Use this to understand Redis dependency load profile and correlate command spikes with latency or failure patterns.

title: Job Worker
